title: The Blind Men and the Elephant

content.text: A long, long time ago, in a faraway land, there lived six blind men who were the best of friends.
They were very curious and loved to learn new things.
One day, they heard about a magnificent elephant that had been brought to their town.
The elephant was said to be the biggest and wisest animal in all the land.
The six blind men were so excited to learn about the elephant that they decided to go and visit it.
They walked to the place where the elephant was kept, their canes tapping on the ground as they went.
When they arrived, they asked the elephant's keeper if they could touch the elephant and learn about it.
The keeper said, "Of course, you can touch the elephant, but be gentle and respectful." The first blind man reached out and touched the elephant's side.
"Oh, the elephant is like a wall," he said.
"It's big and flat and rough."  The second blind man touched the elephant's tusk.
"No, no, my friend," he said. "The elephant is like a spear.
It's sharp and pointed."  The third blind man touched the elephant's trunk. "I don't think so," he said.
"The elephant is like a snake. It's long and wiggly."  The fourth blind man touched the elephant's knee.
"Not at all," he said. "The elephant is like a tree.
It's sturdy and strong."  The fifth blind man touched the elephant's ear. "I disagree," he said.
"The elephant is like a fan. It's soft and flappy."  The sixth blind man touched the elephant's tail.
"You're all wrong," he said. "The elephant is like a rope.
It's long and thin."  The six blind men stood there, each one convinced that their own idea of the elephant was the correct one.
They argued and debated, each one trying to convince the others that they were right.
The elephant's keeper watched them with a smile.
He knew that the elephant was all of those things and more.
He knew that the elephant was a magnificent creature with many different parts, each one special in its own way.
As the six blind men continued to argue, the elephant began to move.
It swung its trunk and flapped its ears. It stomped its foot and swished its tail.
The six blind men were startled and stepped back, their canes tapping wildly as they tried to get out of the way.
As they stood there, catching their breath, the elephant's keeper said, "You see, my friends, the elephant is a very big and complicated creature.
It's not just one thing, it's many things all at once."  The six blind men thought about this for a moment.
They realized that they had each only touched a small part of the elephant, and that they had missed the bigger picture.
They nodded to each other, and then they all reached out and touched the elephant again, this time trying to imagine the whole creature, not just one part.
As they stood there, touching the elephant and imagining its greatness, the six blind men smiled at each other.
They knew that they had learned something new and special, something that they would never forget.
And with that, the six blind men said goodbye to the elephant and its keeper, and they walked away, their canes tapping in unison as they went.
